last_updated: 2026-07-09 (Session 5: Tokenizer efficiency across 48 models — merged original S5 + expansion across 25 new models. Session 6: Output verbosity across 24 models. Session 6b: Compression experiments for DS V4 Flash/Pro with 5 methods.)

primary.name: OpenCode Zen
primary.markup: Zero — passes through provider cost + processing fee

complex.label: Complex
complex.role: Hardest problems — architecture, audits, planning from scratch

daily.label: Daily
daily.role: Strong daily driver — general coding, reasoning

taskrunner.label: Taskrunner
taskrunner.role: Fast routine tasks — implementation, refactoring, reviews

deepseek-v4-flash.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash
deepseek-v4-flash.new_features: Multi-Token Prediction, MoE 284B/13B, Deep EP optimization, 1M context
deepseek-v4-flash.specialty: Cost-sensitive daily coding, general-purpose agent work
deepseek-v4-flash.strategy_note: Your daily driver on Zen. Best value in the market. No reason to switch within tier.

deepseek-v4-flash-max.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash Max

swe_bench_verified.source: SWE-bench Verified leaderboard via BenchLM.ai (Jul 2026). Previously 75.8 — corrected 3.2 pts up.

deepseek-v4-flash-max.new_features: Same as Flash, Max serving config for best throughput
deepseek-v4-flash-max.specialty: Your Max variant choice on OpenRouter. Same model, tuned serving.
deepseek-v4-flash-max.strategy_note: Your secondary daily on OpenRouter. Comparable to Zen Flash.

deepseek-v4-pro.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
deepseek-v4-pro.new_features: 1.6T MoE, 1M context, strong reasoning at low cost
deepseek-v4-pro.specialty: Stronger reasoning than Flash, good for complex coding tasks
deepseek-v4-pro.strategy_note: Strong Taskrunner. Cheaper than GLM 5.2 ($3.48 vs $4.40 out) but lower AAII (44 vs 51). Good when GLM is overkill. Warning: 4× thinking tax inflates effective output cost to $17.40/M (5× $3.48), jumping from Taskrunner to Daily tier.

deepseek-v4-pro-max.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro Max
deepseek-v4-pro-max.new_features: Same as Pro, Max serving config
deepseek-v4-pro-max.specialty: Pro quality at budget pricing on OpenRouter
deepseek-v4-pro-max.strategy_note: Remarkable value — $1/M out for 44 AAII on OpenRouter. Watch for price changes.

deepseek-v4-flash-free.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash Free
deepseek-v4-flash-free.new_features: Same as Flash, free tier (limited time, data may train model)
deepseek-v4-flash-free.specialty: Best free model. Same capability as paid Flash.
deepseek-v4-flash-free.strategy_note: Limited availability. Data may train DeepSeek. Fine for experimentation.

claude-fable-5.name: Claude Fable 5
claude-fable-5.new_features: Anthropic's newest frontier. #1 Arena ELO (1509). #1 Arena Agent (14.06% win rate).
claude-fable-5.specialty: Absolute frontier — planning, architecture, hardest problems
claude-fable-5.strategy_note: Overkill for daily use vs your stack. Consider for critical planning/audit work if GLM 5.2 isn't enough.

claude-opus-4-8.name: Claude Opus 4.8
claude-opus-4-8.new_features: Thinking mode available. Strong agentic coding. 1M context.
claude-opus-4-8.strategy_note: Strongest Claude before Fable 5. Half the price of Fable on output. Consider if GLM 5.2 underperforms on a specific task.

claude-opus-4-7.name: Claude Opus 4.7

swe_bench_pro.source: SWE-bench Pro leaderboard (Jul 2026). Flagged: possible memorization contamination.

claude-opus-4-7.new_features: Previous Opus gen. Still strong. Same pricing as 4.8.
claude-opus-4-7.strategy_note: Same price as 4.8 but slightly lower capability. Prefer 4.8 if both available on Zen.

claude-opus-4-6.name: Claude Opus 4.6
claude-opus-4-6.new_features: Previous Opus gen. Still on Zen. Replaced by Opus 4.7 and 4.8.
claude-opus-4-6.strategy_note: Weaker than 4.7 and 4.8 at the same price ($25/M out). No reason to choose over newer Opus models.

claude-sonnet-5.name: Claude Sonnet 5
claude-sonnet-5.new_features: Adaptive Reasoning (Low/Medium/High/XHigh/Max). Very verbose — 300M output tokens. Strong Arena presence.
claude-sonnet-5.specialty: Best daily driver at this price. Matches Opus 4.7 on AAII at 60% less cost.
claude-sonnet-5.strategy_note: AAII 53 at $10/M out is exceptional. If you ever need a stronger daily than Flash, this is the upgrade path. Consider for complex one-off tasks where GLM is unavailable.

claude-sonnet-4-6.name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
claude-sonnet-4-6.specialty: Strong daily driver, one gen behind Sonnet 5
claude-sonnet-4-6.strategy_note: Sonnet 5 is cheaper ($10 vs $15 out) and stronger. No reason to use 4.6 if 5 is available.

claude-haiku-4-5.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
claude-haiku-4-5.strategy_note: Good but DeepSeek V4 Pro ($3.48 out at AAII 44) beats it on value-to-capability ratio.

gpt-5-5.name: GPT-5.5

swe_bench_verified.source: OpenAI self-reported. Vals.ai independent evaluation shows 82.6. 88.7 stored per OpenAI's claim; 82.6 is the validated independent score.

gpt-5-5.new_features: Multimodal (text+image), 922K context, xhigh reasoning mode. Codex CLI agent scores #1 on Terminal-Bench.
gpt-5-5.specialty: Top-tier agentic coding. Best Terminal-Bench score.
gpt-5-5.strategy_note: Best raw coding benchmark scores. Very expensive at $30/M out. Worth it if Terminal-Bench tasks match your workload. Otherwise, overpriced for your stack.

gpt-5-5-pro.name: GPT-5.5 Pro
gpt-5-5-pro.new_features: Highest-tier OpenAI. Extreme pricing. Use only when nothing else works.
gpt-5-5-pro.strategy_note: $180/M out. Strictly for problems no other model can solve. Unlikely to be worth it for your workflow.

gpt-5-4.name: GPT-5.4
gpt-5-4.new_features: Strong all-rounder. Replaced by GPT-5.5 for frontier work.
gpt-5-4.strategy_note: Solid daily ($15 out, AAII 51) but Sonnet 5 beats it on price ($10 out) and capability (AAII 53).

gpt-5-4-mini.name: GPT-5.4 Mini
gpt-5-4-mini.strategy_note: GLM 5.2 ($4.40 out, AAII 51) is cheaper and vastly more capable. No reason to use Mini.

gpt-5-4-nano.name: GPT-5.4 Nano
gpt-5-4-nano.new_features: Smallest GPT-5.4. Cheap but limited capability.
gpt-5-4-nano.strategy_note: DeepSeek V4 Flash ($0.28 out, AAII 40) is cheaper AND stronger. Nano is only useful if you specifically need an OpenAI model.

gpt-5-nano.name: GPT-5 Nano
gpt-5-nano.new_features: Oldest GPT variant. Very cheap, very limited.
gpt-5-nano.strategy_note: Worse than DeepSeek V4 Flash in every way except cost ($0.40 vs $0.28 out — wait, Flash is cheaper). No reason to use.

gpt-5-2.name: GPT-5.2
gpt-5-2.new_features: Older GPT. Replaced by GPT-5.3 Codex and GPT-5.4. Still on Zen.
gpt-5-2.strategy_note: GPT-5.4 (AAII 51, $15 out) and GPT-5.3 Codex Spark ($14 out) both beat it. Only relevant as a pricing baseline.

gpt-5-3-codex-spark.name: GPT-5.3 Codex Spark
gpt-5-3-codex-spark.strategy_note: GPT-5.4 ($15 out) or Sonnet 5 ($10 out) are likely better at similar or lower cost.

gemini-3-1-pro.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro

aa_intelligence_index.source: AA v4.1 (top tier, tied with Claude Opus 4.7)

gemini-3-1-pro.specialty: Google ecosystem, multimodal tasks, long context
gemini-3-1-pro.strategy_note: Good daily driver. Terminal-Bench 70.7% is respectable. If you work with video/audio, worth considering. Otherwise, GLM 5.2 or Sonnet 5 are better values.

gemini-3-5-flash.name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
gemini-3-5-flash.new_features: Latest Gemini Flash. Fast, cheap, 1M context, multimodal.
gemini-3-5-flash.strategy_note: DeepSeek V4 Flash ($0.28 out, AAII 40) is cheaper. GLM 5.2 ($4.40 out, AAII 51) is stronger. Gemini Flash fills a niche if you need Google's multimodal capabilities.

gemini-3-flash.name: Gemini 3 Flash
gemini-3-flash.new_features: Older Gemini Flash. Still available on Zen.
gemini-3-flash.strategy_note: Superceded by 3.5 Flash. DeepSeek V4 Flash is cheaper and stronger.

glm-5-2.name: GLM 5.2
glm-5-2.new_features: 753B/40B MoE, MIT license, #1 open-weights model, fastest at 208.6 tok/s. #9 Arena Agent (6.62% win rate). #2 Arena WebDev (1584 ELO).
glm-5-2.specialty: Your complex planning/audit model. Open frontier intelligence at a fraction of proprietary cost.
glm-5-2.strategy_note: Your go-to for complex work. AAII 51 at $4.40/M out is unmatched value in its tier. Check for GLM 5.3 in every appraisal.

kimi-k2-7-code.name: Kimi K2.7 Code
kimi-k2-7-code.new_features: Code-specialized Kimi. 1T/32B MoE. Slow but capable. Optimized for direct response — negligible thinking tokens.
kimi-k2-7-code.specialty: Code generation, Chinese-language tasks, open-weight coding
kimi-k2-7-code.strategy_note: At $4/M out with AAII 42 and 46 tok/s, it's slower and less capable than GLM 5.2 ($4.40 out, AAII 51, 208 tok/s). Pass.

kimi-k2-6.name: Kimi K2.6
kimi-k2-6.new_features: Previous Kimi gen. Same price as K2.7 Code but weaker.
kimi-k2-6.strategy_note: K2.7 Code is better at the same price. No reason to use K2.6.

grok-4-5.name: Grok 4.5
grok-4-5.new_features: BRAND NEW (released today!). AAII 54 — #4 overall. Most concise frontier (60M output tokens).
grok-4-5.specialty: Concise frontier intelligence. Complex reasoning at taskrunner pricing.
grok-4-5.strategy_note: **ALERT**: AAII 54 at $6/M out is the best value in this appraisal. Complex-level intelligence (beats Opus 4.8) at Taskrunner pricing. Only 500K context. Worth evaluating as a GLM 5.2 alternative for complex work.

grok-build-0-1.name: Grok Build 0.1
grok-build-0-1.new_features: New xAI build model. Cheap. Unknown benchmarks.
grok-build-0-1.strategy_note: Too new to evaluate. Re-check in next appraisal cycle.

qwen3-7-max.name: Qwen3.7 Max
qwen3-7-max.new_features: Alibaba's largest Qwen. Competitive with mid-tier models.
qwen3-7-max.strategy_note: SWE-bench Pro 60.6% at $7.50/M out is competitive. Still, GLM 5.2 (62.1% at $4.40) is better value.

qwen3-7-plus.name: Qwen3.7 Plus
qwen3-7-plus.new_features: Cheaper Qwen variant. Decent for budget work.
qwen3-7-plus.strategy_note: SWE-bench Pro 57.6% and TB 70.3% at $1.60/M out is strong value. Best budget coding option after DS Flash.

minimax-m3.name: MiniMax M3
minimax-m3.strategy_note: SWE-bench Pro 59.0% / Verified 80.5% at $1.20/M out. Incredible budget value after DS Flash.

mimo-v2-5-free.name: MiMo-V2.5 Free
mimo-v2-5-free.new_features: Free MiniMax model. Limited availability (data may train model).
mimo-v2-5-free.strategy_note: DeepSeek V4 Flash Free is stronger. Only use if DS Free is unavailable.

big-pickle.name: Big Pickle
big-pickle.new_features: Stealth model. Free on Zen. Provider uses data for training.
big-pickle.strategy_note: Free but data trains the model. Only use for non-sensitive experiments.

north-mini-code-free.name: North Mini Code Free
north-mini-code-free.new_features: Free code model. Privacy warning — data trains Cohere models.
north-mini-code-free.strategy_note: Privacy warning: Cohere uses data for training. Skip unless you need a free model and DS Flash Free is down.

nemotron-3-ultra-free.name: Nemotron 3 Ultra Free
nemotron-3-ultra-free.new_features: NVIDIA free trial model. Limited availability.
nemotron-3-ultra-free.strategy_note: NVIDIA trial terms — do not submit personal data. Only for curiosity.

o4-mini.name: o4-mini
o4-mini.new_features: Fast, cost-efficient OpenAI reasoning model. Succeeded by GPT-5 Mini.
o4-mini.strategy_note: SWE-bench Verified 68.1% at $4.40/M out. Strong value reasoning model. Beats DeepSeek V4 Pro on pure SWE-bench (68.1% vs 55.4%) at similar cost.

o3-mini.name: o3-mini

swe_bench_verified.source: BenchLM.ai (Jul 2026). Previously 65.0 from Jan 2025 model card — stale data corrected.

o3-mini.new_features: Predecessor to o4-mini. Older reasoning model.
o3-mini.strategy_note: o4-mini is better at the same price. No reason to use o3-mini if o4-mini is available.

deepseek-r1.name: DeepSeek R1

swe_bench_verified.source: DeepSeek R1 model card, SWE-bench Verified

deepseek-r1.new_features: Original reasoning pioneer. 671B MoE. Open weights. RL-trained reasoning.
deepseek-r1.specialty: Reasoning, math, coding — the model that kicked off the reasoning era
deepseek-r1.strategy_note: Aged but historically important. DeepSeek V4 Pro ($3.48 out, AAII 44) and o4-mini ($4.40 out, SWE-bench 68.1%) both outperform R1 in their respective strengths. R1 is superseded within the family.

deepseek-v3-2.name: DeepSeek V3.2
deepseek-v3-2.new_features: Latest V3 generation. Strong reasoning and agent performance. Open weights.
deepseek-v3-2.specialty: Budget powerful coding, predecessor to V4 Flash
deepseek-v3-2.strategy_note: SWE-bench Verified 70.0% at $0.34/M out is incredible value. Cheaper than V4 Flash ($0.28 out + cache) with very similar capability. Worth considering as a Flash alternative if OpenRouter rates hold.

deepseek-chat-v3.name: DeepSeek Chat V3
deepseek-chat-v3.new_features: Original V3 chat model. Superseded by V3.2.
deepseek-chat-v3.strategy_note: Superseded by DeepSeek V3.2 ($0.34 out, SWE-bench Verified 70.0%) and V4 Flash ($0.28 out). No reason to use.

gemini-2-5-pro.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
gemini-2-5-pro.new_features: 1M context, thinking mode, multimodal (text+image+video+audio). Very strong GPQA (86.4%).
gemini-2-5-pro.specialty: Google flagship reasoning, long context, multimodal
gemini-2-5-pro.strategy_note: Strong daily driver option ($10/M out). GPQA 86.4% and Aider-Polyglot 82.2% are competitive with Claude Sonnet 5 ($10 out). Consider if you need Google's multimodal capabilities or 1M context.

llama-3-3-70b.name: Llama 3.3 70B
llama-3-3-70b.new_features: Most popular open-weight model. 70B dense. Strong for self-hosting.
llama-3-3-70b.strategy_note: Good for self-hosting but not competitive on API pricing. DeepSeek V4 Flash ($0.28 out) is cheaper and stronger. Only relevant if you need open weights for a specific deployment constraint.

llama-4-maverick.name: Llama 4 Maverick

swe_bench_verified.source: SWE-bench Verified — NOT in BenchLM.ai top 57 (Jul 2026). Score from Jun 2026, needs live verification. May reflect a different subset or stale data.

llama-4-maverick.new_features: Natively multimodal MoE. 1M context. 128 experts. Open weights.
llama-4-maverick.strategy_note: SWE-bench Verified 75.0% at $0.60/M out is exceptional value for an open-weight model. AAII 14 is misleading (AA's methodology penalizes MoE). Its real-world coding ability is much higher than AAII suggests.

mistral-large-3.name: Mistral Large 3

gpqa_diamond.source: Mistral Large 3 model card (5-shot no CoT)

mistral-large-3.new_features: Mistral flagship. 675B MoE. Open weights. Competitive with DeepSeek V3.2.
mistral-large-3.strategy_note: MMLU-Pro 85.46% is strong, but agentic coding benchmarks are missing. DeepSeek V4 Flash and V3.2 are cheaper with better coding scores. Consider if you need Mistral's European hosting.

codestral.name: Codestral
codestral.new_features: Code-specialized model. 80+ programming languages. 256K context. Superseded by Codestral 2.0.
codestral.strategy_note: Weak SWE-bench Verified (40%) vs DeepSeek V4 Flash. Only relevant if you want a dedicated code model on Mistral's platform.

command-a.name: Command A
command-a.new_features: Enterprise-focused. 256K context. Strong tool use and RAG. Open weights (non-commercial).
command-a.strategy_note: Expensive ($10/M out) for its capability level. Cohere's strength is enterprise RAG and tool use, not raw coding. Skip unless you need Cohere's platform.

phi-4.name: Phi-4
phi-4.new_features: Small but mighty. 14B dense. MIT license. Uses tiktoken tokenizer.
phi-4.specialty: Ultra-cheap open-weight, local deployment, low latency
phi-4.strategy_note: At $0.14/M out with only 16K context, it's a niche model for local deployment or ultra-low-cost simple tasks. DeepSeek V4 Flash dominates this tier on API.

nova-pro.name: Amazon Nova Pro
nova-pro.new_features: AWS flagship multimodal model. 300K context. Vision, video, tool use.
nova-pro.strategy_note: Weak coding benchmarks (LiveCodeBench 23.3%) vs similarly-priced alternatives. GLM 5.2 ($4.40 out, AAII 51) is dramatically stronger. Only relevant if you're already in the AWS Bedrock ecosystem.

perplexity-sonar-pro-search.name: Perplexity Sonar Pro Search
perplexity-sonar-pro-search.new_features: Search-augmented model. Real-time web search + answer generation. 200K context.
perplexity-sonar-pro-search.strategy_note: Expensive ($15/M out) for a search model. Use only when you need verified citations with LLM synthesis. Not a general coding model.

perplexity-sonar-pro.name: Perplexity Sonar Pro
perplexity-sonar-pro.new_features: Search-augmented model. Predecessor to Sonar Pro Search.
perplexity-sonar-pro.strategy_note: Superseded by Sonar Pro Search at same price. Same tokenizer.

nova-premier-v1.name: Amazon Nova Premier
nova-premier-v1.strategy_note: Very verbose tokenizer (E=2.24). Nova Pro ($3.20 out, E=1.87) is cheaper AND more efficient. Nova Premier's $12.50/M out with 20% more tokens per word makes it poor value vs alternatives.
